info.name: Padel Access Control API
info.description:
API collection for the Padel Facility Access Control System.

## Setup
1. Go to the **Variables** tab of this collection
2. Set `base_url` to your server URL (e.g. `https://your-host.example.com`)
3. Set `api_key` to your API key

## Endpoints
- **System** — Health, audit logs, settings, operating mode, reboot
- **Codes** — CRUD for access codes (incl. list-with-status)
- **Control** — Remote door unlock and light control

item.name: System

request.description:
Check if the system is running. No authentication required.

Returns status and uptime in seconds.

query.description: Number of log entries to return (max 500)
Filter by event type: DOOR_OPEN, LIGHT_ON, LIGHT_OFF, CODE_FAIL, REMOTE_DOOR, REMOTE_LIGHT, SETTINGS_UPDATE, MODE_CHANGE, SYSTEM_REBOOT

request.description:
Retrieve audit log entries. Optionally filter by event type.

**Event types:**
- `DOOR_OPEN` — Door was unlocked (keypad or button)
- `LIGHT_ON` / `LIGHT_OFF` — Lights toggled
- `CODE_FAIL` — Invalid code entered
- `REMOTE_DOOR` — Door unlocked via API
- `REMOTE_LIGHT` — Lights controlled via API
- `SETTINGS_UPDATE` — Runtime settings changed via API
- `MODE_CHANGE` — System operating mode changed
- `SYSTEM_REBOOT` — Reboot requested via API

Read the current live values of every runtime-mutable setting.

Reflects the merged view of `.env` defaults plus any overrides persisted in `data/runtime_settings.json`.

Update one or more runtime settings. Send only the keys you want to change — partial bodies are supported. Changes are persisted to `data/runtime_settings.json` and survive a reboot.

**Mutable keys:**
- `door_unlock_duration` (int 1–60) — door unlock pulse duration in seconds
- `mask_code_input` (bool) — show entered digits as `*` on the OLED
- `buzzer_enabled` (bool) — runtime buzzer toggle
- `door_open_alarm_enabled` (bool) — buzzer alarm if door stays open
- `door_open_alarm_seconds` (int 5–600) — grace period before the alarm fires
- `display_idle_text` / `display_idle_subtext` (string ≤40)
- `app_lang` (`EN` or `KA`)
- `log_level` (`DEBUG` / `INFO` / `WARNING` / `ERROR` / `CRITICAL`)
- `code_length` (int 4–8)

Returns 422 for unknown keys, out-of-range values, or attempts to set `system_mode` (use `POST /api/system/mode` instead).

Hardware-shape settings (GPIO pins, port, API key, door sensor enable) cannot be changed at runtime — edit `.env` and reboot.

Get the current operating mode.

**Modes:**
- `normal` — keypad active, exit button works, remote unlock pulses the door for `door_unlock_duration` seconds
- `keypad_disabled` — keypad ignored; exit button + remote unlock still work
- `free` — door relay held energized indefinitely (door always open), all light relays held on, keypad ignored, buzzer suppressed, door-open alarm suppressed

Switch the operating mode. The new mode is persisted so the system comes back up in the same mode after a reboot.

**Mode value must be one of:** `normal`, `keypad_disabled`, `free`

**Free mode side effects on entry:**
- Door relay energized (door stays open)
- All light relays energized indefinitely (no scheduled turn-off)
- Door-open alarm cancelled and suppressed
- Display shows `FREE MODE` / `door open`
- `POST /api/control/door` returns `{"status":"free_mode_noop"}` instead of pulsing

**On leaving free mode:** door relay drops, all lights off, display returns to idle.

Reboot the Pi. Requires `confirm: true` in the body to guard against accidental requests, and is rate-limited to **1 call per minute**.

Returns `202 Accepted` immediately and the actual reboot fires from a worker thread one second later, so the response has time to flush. The systemd service comes back up automatically and restores the persisted operating mode.

Audit-logs `SYSTEM_REBOOT`.

item.description: Health, audit logs, runtime settings, operating mode, and reboot.

item.name: Access Codes

request.description:
Create a new access code for the facility.

**Fields:**
- `code` — The numeric code the user will enter on the keypad
- `light_ids` — Which light zones to activate: `[1]`, `[2]`, or `[1, 2]`
- `valid_from` — Start of validity window (UTC)
- `valid_until` — End of validity window (UTC). Lights auto-off at this time
- `label` — Human-readable label (e.g. customer name, court number)
- `max_uses` — (optional) Max number of uses. Set to `1` for one-time codes. Omit for unlimited.

Auto-generate a random numeric code. Perfect for booking systems.

**Fields:**
- `light_ids` — Which light zones to activate
- `valid_from` / `valid_until` — Validity window
- `label` — Human-readable label
- `max_uses` — Max uses (default: 1 = one-time code). Set to `null` for unlimited
- `code_length` — Number of digits (default: 6)

Returns the generated code in the response.

query.description: Set to true to only show active (non-deleted) codes

request.description: List all access codes. Use `active_only=true` to filter out deactivated codes.

query.description: Set to true to only show codes with is_active=true (a soft-delete flag, not a status filter)

request.description:
List every access code with its derived status, dates and uses_remaining computed in one round-trip — saves dashboard clients from calling `/api/codes/check` per row.

**Status values per row:**
- `active` — within the validity window and has uses left
- `not_yet_valid` — `valid_from` is in the future
- `expired` — past `valid_until`
- `used` — `max_uses` reached and the row was deactivated
- `inactive` — soft-deleted via DELETE

`uses_remaining` is `null` for unlimited codes and `0` for fully-used ones.

Look up a code by its value and get its current status. The code is sent in the request body so it never appears in URL paths or access logs.

**Possible statuses:**
- `active` — Code is valid and ready to use
- `not_yet_valid` — Code exists but validity window hasn't started
- `expired` — Validity window has passed
- `used` — One-time code already used
- `inactive` — Code was deactivated
- `not_found` — Code doesn't exist

Also returns `uses_remaining` for limited-use codes.
Get details of a specific access code by ID.

Update an existing access code. All fields are optional — only include the fields you want to change.

**Updatable fields:**
- `code`, `light_ids`, `valid_from`, `valid_until`, `label`, `is_active`

Soft-delete an access code. The code will no longer work on the keypad and any scheduled light-off jobs are cancelled.

The code remains in the database with `is_active: false`.

item.description:
Create, list, update, and deactivate access codes.

Each code unlocks the door and activates the specified light zones for the duration of the validity window.

item.name: Remote Control

request.description: Remotely unlock the door for the configured duration (default 5 seconds).

Turn on light zones. The `until` field is required — lights will automatically turn off at that time.

**Light zones:** `1` and `2`
Turn off specific light zones.
Emergency off — turn off all light zones immediately.

Get current status of the door lock and all light zones.

Shows whether each relay is on/off and when lights are scheduled to turn off.

item.description:
Remote control for the door lock and light zones.

Use these endpoints to unlock the door or control lights without a keypad code.
